title: Critical Validation of DA9 Conclusions

v0_ema.finding: EMA improves DiT-x0 on ALL 6 encoders (every delta positive). Mean improvement +0.0037. Largest on dino (+0.0072), vit (+0.0068), clip (+0.0047). Gate G1 PASSES (3/6 encoders improve >0.002).
v0_ema.caveat: MLP was NOT EMA-trained. Advantage may reflect regularization, not diffusion. Report as sensitivity analysis with asterisk.

v1_stat_validation.finding: Both pre-registered primary targets are HIGHLY SIGNIFICANT at scene level with FDR correction. DiT-x0 (EMA) beats MLP-residual on hardest scenarios (Q1/h4) for 3 encoders.

dino_vits14_Q1_h4.note: Fisher p assumes seed independence (overstates significance). Effect size d=0.25 is the more honest metric.

clip_b32_Q1_h4.note: Gap (+0.0036) overlaps EMA gain for clip (+0.0047); may be entirely EMA regularization.

v1_stat_validation.method: Per-scene paired t-test (N~130 scenes per quartile). Primary: pooled scene-level test (average per-scene gaps across seeds, then one t-test). Secondary: Fisher combined p across 3 seeds with BH FDR correction (overstates significance due to shared test set across seeds).
v1_stat_validation.caveat: DiT used EMA weights; MLP did not. EMA alone buys +0.0037 mean (up to +0.0072 dino), which overlaps or exceeds the reported gaps. Effect sizes are small (d=0.12-0.25). Fisher p-values assume seed independence but seeds share the test set.

v2_difficulty_diagnostics.finding: Copy-baseline quartiles are well-spread and monotonic on all encoders. Difficulty axis is valid. Q1 ranges from 0.73 (vit_s16 h4) to 0.97 (vq_track h4).

v3_action_variance.finding: dino_vits14 shows positive DiT gap (+0.0098) in highest action-variance quartile (AV-Q4). This is consistent: DiT helps when actions change dynamically. Other encoders show DiT gap closer to zero in AV-Q4 but not positive.
v3_action_variance.notable: dino_vits14 AV-Q4 h4: gap=+0.0098 (DiT wins on most dynamic scenes)

m4_perstep_horizon.finding: MLP-residual wins at EVERY SINGLE STEP in h=8/h=16 across all encoders. No hidden near-step DiT advantage. The gap is largest at step 1 and shrinks toward the last step.

gate_g4_decision.decision: G2 passes nominally but is confounded by EMA asymmetry (DiT used EMA, MLP did not). The reported gaps overlap EMA regularization gains, and Fisher p-values overstate significance due to shared test sets across seeds. G3 failed. The DiT signal is at best narrow (Q1/h4 only) and may be partially or fully attributable to EMA. Lead with the negative result.

overall_finding: MLP-residual dominates DiT-x0 on aggregate CosSim across all encoders, all quartiles except Q1/h4, and all longer horizons. A narrow positive signal for DiT appears on the hardest scenarios (Q1/h4) for 3 encoders (dino +0.0066, clip +0.0036, vit +0.0029), but this signal is confounded: (1) DiT used EMA while MLP did not, and EMA alone buys +0.0037 mean (up to +0.0072 dino), overlapping the reported gaps; (2) Fisher p-values overstate significance because seeds share the test set; (3) effect sizes are small (Cohen d = 0.12-0.25). The Q1/h4 signal motivates a hypothesis that diffusion may help on high-uncertainty scenes, but is not sufficient to claim an established advantage.
report_framing: Scenario B -- negative result with EMA-confounded narrow positive signal as hypothesis
